news.headline: Innospec reports Q4 revenue of $466.8 million, down 6%. Net loss of $70.4 million with operating income growth in Performance Chemicals. $25.7 million cash generated with net cash of $289.2 million. Non-cash charge of $155.6 million on UK pension scheme buyout. GAAP loss per share $2.80; adjusted non-GAAP EPS $1.41. Total 2024 revenue $1.85 billion, down 5%. Net income $35.6 million. Adjusted EBITDA $225.2 million for the year. CEO optimistic about growth opportunities.
news.summary: Innospec Inc. reported their financial results for Q4 2024 and full year ended December 31, 2024. The total revenues for Q4 were $466.8 million, a 6% decrease from the same period last year. The net loss was $70.4 million, mainly due to a $155.6 million non-cash settlement charge on a UK pension scheme buyout. Adjusted EBITDA for Q4 was $56.6 million. For the full year, total revenues were $1.85 billion, a 5% decrease from the previous year. Net income for 2024 was $35.6 million, impacted by the UK pension scheme buyout. Adjusted EBITDA for the year was $225.2 million, up 4% from 2023. Performance Chemicals operating income increased by 14%, Fuel Specialties operating income was up by 7%, while Oilfield Services saw lower production chemical activity in Latin America. The company generated $25.7 million cash from operations and had a net cash balance of $289.2 million. Adjusted non-GAAP EPS was $1.41 for Q4 and $5.92 for the full year, showing resilience despite challenges.

Paralyzed Veterans of America awards winners at 8th annual Wheelchair Rugby Invitational in Louisville, KY.
news.summary: In Louisville, Kentucky, on February 18, 2025, the eighth annual Wheelchair Rugby Invitational organized by Paralyzed Veterans of America took place. The event saw the presentation of the Code of Honor Cup to the victors of each division. Among the notable champions was the team WWAR.

news.headline: Hamilton Beach Brands Holding Company declared a quarterly dividend of $0.115 per share for both Class A and Class B Common Stock.
news.summary: In Glen Allen, Virginia, on February 18, 2025, Hamilton Beach Brands Holding Company (NYSE: HBB) shared that their Board of Directors approved a standard cash dividend of $0.115 per share. This dividend applies to both Class A and Class B Common Stock holders.

news.headline: Health Union launches new online community for those with opioid use disorder (OUD). Feb 18, 2025. Expands support services.
news.summary: A new online community focused on providing support for individuals dealing with opioid use disorder (OUD) has recently been introduced by Health Union, a prominent digital health organization. This initiative serves as an additional offering from Health Union, recognized for its expertise in online community-based activation and data. The community aims to assist those affected by OUD and is part of Health Union's ongoing efforts to address the prevalent issue of opioid misuse.

news.headline: Vascarta's transdermal curcumin gel may revolutionize sickle cell treatment, as per studies in PNAS Nexus.
news.summary: In a recent publication from the Proceedings of the National Academy of Sciences (PNAS) Nexus journal, Goel et al. conducted studies on humanized sickle cell mice utilizing Vascarta's transdermal curcumin gel known as "VAS-101." The research, conducted in Summit, N.J., on Feb. 18, 2025, shed light on the effects of this topical treatment, showcasing its potential benefits in managing sickle cell disease. This development highlights the promising role of Vascarta's formulation in targeting this condition, with significant implications for future therapeutic approaches.

news.headline: Capital Southwest appoints Michael Sarner as new CEO, with Chris Rehberger as CFO and Tabitha Geiger as CCO. Bowen Diehl steps down.
news.summary: Michael Sarner has been announced as the new President and Chief Executive Officer, succeeding Bowen Diehl. Additionally, Chris Rehberger has been promoted to Chief Financial Officer, Treasurer, and Secretary, while Tabitha Geiger has been promoted to Chief Compliance Officer. These changes mark a significant transition in leadership within the organization.

news.headline: Devon Energy beats Q4 earnings with $1.16 per share, reports $1.7B operating cash flow, raises quarterly dividend.
news.summary: Devon Energy Corp released its fourth-quarter financial report, surpassing analyst predictions with earnings of $1.16 per share compared to the expected $1.00. The company saw oil production hit 398,000 barrels per day, exceeding previous estimates by 3%. Operating cash flow stood at $1.7 billion, with free cash flow at $738 million for the quarter. Shareholders received $444 million through dividends and repurchases, while the company bought back $300 million worth of shares. Details of the report can be found on Benzinga.com.

news.headline: Varsity Yearbook's Press Pass Academy starts with 75+ students gaining journalism and media experience at national championships.

The event was the first of its kind and aimed to provide hands-on experience in the field. Varsity Spirit, a division of Varsity Brands known for its leadership in cheerleading, dance team, band apparel, camps, and competitions, organized the event.

news.headline: Duke Energy appoints Katherine Neebe as new CCO, replacing Oscar Suris. Effective Feb. 24, 2025.

Katherine Neebe, the current senior vice president and chief sustainability officer, will take on the role of chief communications officer (CCO) starting from February 24. This move comes as Neebe succeeds Oscar Suris, who held the position of senior vice president and CCO. This internal restructuring is aimed at enhancing Duke Energy's communication strategies and further solidifying its position in the industry.

news.summary: The SOL to ETH ratio, which hit all-time highs in January, is now seeing a reversal as a result of memecoin controversies.

news.headline: AEP Texas warns of severe Arctic cold front setting in Feb 18-20, with temps dipping below Jan levels, impacting communities.
news.summary: Important note for Texas residents: AEP Texas is urging customers to prepare for an upcoming Arctic cold front from February 18 to February 20, 2025. Temperatures are anticipated to plummet significantly below previous levels, threatening severe cold conditions across the area. Take necessary precautions to stay safe during this harsh freeze.

news.headline: Coeur Mining reports 3.6M oz of gold, 270.5M oz of silver for 2024 reserves, reflecting 22% increase in gold reserves at Kensington.

The numbers also include silver and gold ounces acquired recently as part of their exploration program at the Palmarejo operation.

news.headline: Starbox registers trademarks in Philippines, incl. "Starbox," "StarboxAI," and "StarboxChat," part of regional growth strategy.

This move signifies a significant advancement in Starbox's regional expansion plan, demonstrating its dedication to establishing a solid foothold in Southeast Asia. The Philippines, with its growing population, serves as a pivotal market in the company's growth strategy, in alignment with its overarching expansion objectives.

The preferred stock dividends apply to depositary shares, where each represents a 1/1,000th interest in a share of preferred stock. This announcement highlights Athene's commitment to its shareholders and signifies the company's financial health.

news.headline: Teva to discuss Duvakitug study results at conference call on Feb 24, 2025, from the 20th ECCO. Key leaders to present.

news.headline: Bumble, Inc. (NASDAQ:BMBL) Q4 Report: Revenue $261.6M, Down from $273.64M last year. Total Paying Users fall by 57,000. App Revenue dips by 3.8% to $212.4M.
news.summary: Dating app company Bumble, Inc. (NASDAQ:BMBL) released its Q4 results, surpassing analyst expectations with a revenue of $261.6 million, a drop from $273.64 million in the same period last year. Total Paying Users decreased by 57,000 from the previous quarter. Bumble App Revenue also saw a decline of 3.8% to $212.4 million, compared to $220.7 million, with an adverse impact of $1.7 million from currency movements.

news.headline: The Children's Place appoints Rhys Summerton to Board of Directors; he'll serve until 2025 annual meeting.
news.summary: Secaucus, New Jersey, on February 18, 2025 - The Children’s Place, Inc. (Nasdaq: PLCE), the leading pure-play children’s specialty retailer in North America with a range of brands across various channels, has announced the appointment of Rhys Summerton to its Board of Directors and as a member of the Audit Committee. Mr. Summerton will serve until the Company's 2025 annual stockholders meeting. Ms. Summerton, an independent director, meets the criteria as an “audit committee financial expert” per SEC and Nasdaq regulations.
